objective: Implement PU-013 by making runtime-card and harness next consume validated Codex runtime evidence packets as cockpit input while preserving runtime-card/v1 as a compact summary and pointer object.
deepModuleBoundary: src/lib/runtime runtime-card ingestion and src/commands/next advisory cockpit metadata
implementationThesis: Use additive input normalization and metadata projection: runtime-card --evidence accepts runtime-evidence-bundle/v1 or codex-runtime-evidence/v1, adapts Codex packets through the existing runtime evidence adapter, and harness next surfaces compact Codex runtime summaries as advisory metadata without executing actions or asserting merge readiness.

currentEvidence.observed: --evidence parses repo-contained JSON and passes it directly into runtime-card construction as evidenceBundle.
currentEvidence.gap: codex-runtime-evidence/v1 packets are not normalized at the CLI boundary.

currentEvidence.observed: adaptCodexRuntimeEvidenceToRuntimeEvidenceBundle maps validated Codex packets into runtime-evidence-bundle/v1.
currentEvidence.gap: the adapter is not wired into runtime-card --evidence ingestion.

currentEvidence.observed: harness next includes normaliseRuntimeCard(runtimeCard) in metadata.
currentEvidence.gap: next tests do not prove Codex runtime summary, stale-state refs, or blocked inputs remain visible as advisory metadata.

currentEvidence.observed: harness next --runtime-card loads artifact paths directly and accepts absolute paths without the repo-boundary and symlink checks used by runtime-card --evidence.
currentEvidence.gap: runtime-card and harness next currently have different artifact trust boundaries.

outOfScope: changing pr-closeout, external-state, review-state, or root-hygiene semantics reserved for later PU units
expanding harness next into executable authority
embedding raw Codex events, prompts, final assistant responses, transcripts, full review bodies, secrets, credentials, or bulky telemetry in runtime-card/v1
claiming merge readiness or goal completion from runtime-card metadata alone
acceptanceCriteria: runtime-card --evidence accepts a repo-contained codex-runtime-evidence/v1 packet and emits a valid runtime-card/v1 with codexRuntime summary fields.
runtime-card --evidence continues to accept runtime-evidence-bundle/v1 artifacts without regression.
harness next --runtime-card enforces the same repo-contained path, traversal, and symlink-safety boundary used by runtime-card --evidence.
malformed Codex runtime packets fail closed with sanitized runtime-card-error/v1 output.
stale or blocked Codex evidence is projected as blockers/advisory metadata and cannot become merge-ready or delivery-ready proof.
harness next --json remains advisory and exposes runtime-card Codex summaries only as metadata when a runtime-card artifact is supplied.
tests prove valid packet, stale packet, malformed packet, and advisory-only unsupported-claim cases.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: intent artifact is syntactically valid JSON before review

automationPlan.name: focused runtime cockpit tests
automationPlan.command: pnpm vitest run src/lib/runtime/*.test.ts src/commands/runtime-card.test.ts src/commands/next*.test.ts
proves: PU-013 runtime-card evidence ingestion, shared artifact path safety, and harness next cockpit projection behavior

automationPlan.name: runtime-card smoke
proves: runtime-card CLI still emits schema-valid local cockpit output

automationPlan.name: harness next smoke
proves: harness next remains a narrow advisory cockpit after metadata changes

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after PU-013 updates

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, implementationThesis, currentEvidence, inScope, outOfScope, guardedPathGlobs, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending

parallelPrTriageStrategy.default: allow a read-only PR triage subagent to monitor PR #305 while the coordinator performs PU-013 intent review and implementation on the active branch
parallelPrTriageStrategy.parallelCodeWorkAllowed: allowed only if PR #305 failures, if any, are classified as unrelated to files changed by PU-013 or are fixed before the PU-013 commit is pushed
blockedWhen: PR #305 reports a failure caused by current branch files that PU-013 would also touch
intent reviewers identify a material cockpit authority or evidence-warehouse risk
implementation would require Codex source mutation or public delivery-truth command exposure
parallelPrTriageStrategy.safeFallback: continue read-only PR triage, fixture design, and intent refinement without touching production runtime code

stopConditions: Stop if adversarial or agent-native intent review reports a material blocker and the intent has not been patched and re-reviewed.
Stop if runtime-card --evidence would need to read evidence outside --repo or follow unsafe symlinks.
Stop if harness next --runtime-card would need to read runtime-card artifacts outside --repo or follow unsafe symlinks.
Stop if runtime-card/v1 would need to embed raw packet bodies instead of refs and summaries.
Stop if harness next metadata would become action authority, merge-readiness proof, or closeout proof.
Stop if a malformed Codex packet can be silently ignored while a success card is emitted.
Stop if PR #305 reports a current-head failure in touched runtime-card or next surfaces that must be fixed before continuing.
rollback: Remove codex-runtime-evidence/v1 detection from runtime-card --evidence and preserve existing runtime-evidence-bundle/v1 ingestion.
Remove any added harness next Codex runtime metadata projection while preserving existing runtime-card metadata.
Keep negative tests for raw payload rejection unless they are superseded by stronger existing validation.
Do not modify or roll back /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex because PU-013 is Harness-only.
assumptions: PU-012 producer bridge has already established a Harness-owned wrapper/import path; PU-013 consumes that path rather than choosing a new producer boundary.
Existing runtime-card validation and codex-runtime-evidence validation are the canonical schemas for this slice.
PR #305 may remain draft or pending while PU-013 proceeds, but no merge-ready claim is allowed without current external claim-support evidence.
